title: Laura Lloyd
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Laura Lloyd
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Born in Minnesota but raised in Texas, I am a product of a very artistic family. I truly believe theatre and dance are in my blood. I graduated from Angelo State University in San Angelo, TX with a BA in Theatre and English. I worked as a professional actor with a regional theatre company (Granbury Opera House) for a year before realizing I wanted to continue my education. I attended Western Illinois University where I received an MFA in Acting with a concentration in Musical Theatre and was also a member of the University Dance Company. After graduating, I worked in several professional theatre companies throughout the United States. I then went home to Texas and taught dance and musical theatre at a private theatre arts school. An opportunity arose to come to Virginia and stage manage and perform as a singer and dancer at Busch Gardens. I set roots in Newport News and began teaching high school theatre and dance. I taught in the Newport News Public School system for 9 years and during that time began adjuncting as a dance instructor at Christopher Newport University. I am now a full time lecturer with the Department of Music, Theatre, and Dance and am very happy to be a part of the CNU family.
education_entries: MFA in Musical Theatre, Western Illinois University
BA in Theatre, Angelo State University
research_interests: Dance in Musical Theatre - there are very few resources for this and I would like to explore this topic more. I am working on an online resource for Broadway Tap. I also plan to continue my training in Elemental Body Alignment for which I am certified (EBA).
teaching_interests: Ballet, Jazz, Musical Theatre Dance Styles, Tap, Acting, Actor's Voice, Actor's Body, Advanced Acting, History of Musical Theatre, Choreography

Presentations: Gregg Lloyd, Laura Lloyd, Musical Theater Educators Alliance Annual Conference, "Are Traditional Program Auditions Discriminatory," New York, New York. (2023)
